req.title: Update patient (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall update patient demographics; historical versions must be preserved. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-028 requires three distinct test angles: (1) a positive EP test confirming a valid clinician PUT stores a new version and preserves history; (2) a negative NEG/DT test confirming a 409 is returned when the supplied versionId conflicts with the current server version; (3) a BVA boundary test probing the minimum and maximum representable birthDate (epoch boundary 1900-01-01 and near-future 2099-12-31) and an integer field at its allowed numeric boundary (e.g. multipleBirthInteger=0). State transition is implicitly covered by the version progression sequence. All tests are self-contained via a session-scoped fixture that creates then deletes a scratch patient, making each test idempotent.

tests.name: test_update_patient_valid_demographics_preserves_history
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician (Bearer token in <clinician_token> fixture)
A Patient resource exists under <base_patient> fixture with a known versionId
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with updated family name and correct If-Match ETag
expected: Response body meta.versionId is incremented by 1
Oldest history entry preserves original family name
trace: R-HLT-028: clinician updates resource -> new version stored
R-HLT-028: historical versions must be preserved
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests

def test_update_patient_valid_demographics_preserves_history(
    clinician_token, base_patient
):
    pid = base_patient["id"]
    old_vid = base_patient["meta"]["versionId"]
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{old_vid}"',
    }
    updated = dict(base_patient)
    updated["name"] = [{"family": "UpdatedFamily", "given": ["Jane"]}]
    put_r = requests.put(
        f"<FHIR_BASE>/Patient/{pid}", json=updated, headers=headers
    )
    assert put_r.status_code == 200, put_r.text
    new_vid = put_r.json()["meta"]["versionId"]
    assert int(new_vid) == int(old_vid) + 1

    hist_r = requests.get(
        f"<FHIR_BASE>/Patient/{pid}/_history",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert hist_r.status_code == 200
    entries = hist_r.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2
    families = [
        n["family"]
        for e in entries
        for n in e.get("resource", {}).get("name", [])
    ]
    assert "OriginalFamily" in families
    assert "UpdatedFamily" in families

tests.name: test_update_patient_version_conflict_returns_409
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician (Bearer token in <clinician_token> fixture)
A Patient resource exists under <base_patient> fixture
A stale versionId (current - 1 or arbitrary wrong value) is used in If-Match
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with If-Match containing a deliberately wrong ETag
expected: Response OperationOutcome issue.code indicates version conflict
trace: R-HLT-028: Error pathway 409 on version conflict
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests

def test_update_patient_version_conflict_returns_409(
    clinician_token, base_patient
):
    pid = base_patient["id"]
    stale_vid = "0"  # guaranteed wrong version
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{stale_vid}"',
    }
    payload = dict(base_patient)
    payload["name"] = [{"family": "ConflictTest"}]
    put_r = requests.put(
        f"<FHIR_BASE>/Patient/{pid}", json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert put_r.status_code == 409, (
        f"Expected 409 but got {put_r.status_code}: {put_r.text}"
    )
    body = put_r.json()
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(
        i.get("code") in ("conflict", "version-conflict") for i in issues
    ), f"No conflict issue code found in: {issues}"

tests.name: test_update_patient_boundary_dates_and_numeric_fields
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician (Bearer token in <clinician_token> fixture)
A Patient resource exists under <base_patient> fixture
The server supports FHIR date type boundaries and integer multipleBirth
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with birthDate=1900-01-01 (minimum epoch boundary) and multipleBirthInteger=0 (zero boundary)
Re-PUT with birthDate=2099-12-31 (upper boundary) and multipleBirthInteger=99 (practical upper boundary)
expected: Returned birthDate equals submitted boundary value in each case
Returned multipleBirthInteger equals submitted boundary value in each case
versionId increments for each successful PUT
trace: R-HLT-028: Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests

def _put_patient(pid, payload, vid, token):
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{vid}"',
    }
    r = requests.put(f"<FHIR_BASE>/Patient/{pid}", json=payload, headers=headers)
    return r

def test_update_patient_boundary_dates_and_numeric_fields(
    clinician_token, base_patient
):
    pid = base_patient["id"]
    current_vid = base_patient["meta"]["versionId"]

    for birth_date, mult_birth in [
        ("1900-01-01", 0),
        ("2099-12-31", 99),
    ]:
        payload = dict(base_patient)
        payload["meta"] = {"versionId": current_vid}
        payload["birthDate"] = birth_date
        payload["multipleBirthInteger"] = mult_birth

        r = _put_patient(pid, payload, current_vid, clinician_token)
        assert r.status_code == 200, (
            f"birthDate={birth_date} mult={mult_birth}: {r.text}"
        )
        body = r.json()
        assert body["birthDate"] == birth_date
        assert body["multipleBirthInteger"] == mult_birth
        new_vid = body["meta"]["versionId"]
        assert int(new_vid) == int(current_vid) + 1
        current_vid = new_vid
        base_patient["meta"]["versionId"] = current_vid

tests.name: test_update_patient_state_transition_version_sequence
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician (Bearer token in <clinician_token> fixture)
A Patient resource exists under <base_patient> fixture with versionId N
actions: PUT #1: valid update -> expect versionId N+1
PUT #2: valid update using versionId N+1 -> expect versionId N+2
GET /_history -> confirm linear version chain N, N+1, N+2
expected: Each successive PUT returns 200 with monotonically increasing versionId
History contains exactly the three version entries in order
Each history entry has distinct lastUpdated timestamps
trace: R-HLT-028: new version stored; history preserved (multi-step state progression)
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests

def test_update_patient_state_transition_version_sequence(
    clinician_token, base_patient
):
    pid = base_patient["id"]
    vid = base_patient["meta"]["versionId"]
    token = clinician_token
    base_url = f"<FHIR_BASE>/Patient/{pid}"
    version_ids = [vid]

    for step, given_name in enumerate(["StepOne", "StepTwo"], start=1):
        payload = dict(base_patient)
        payload["meta"] = {"versionId": vid}
        payload["name"] = [{"family": "StateTest", "given": [given_name]}]
        headers = {
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
            "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
            "If-Match": f'W/"{vid}"',
        }
        r = requests.put(base_url, json=payload, headers=headers)
        assert r.status_code == 200, f"Step {step} failed: {r.text}"
        vid = r.json()["meta"]["versionId"]
        assert int(vid) == int(version_ids[-1]) + 1
        version_ids.append(vid)
        base_patient["meta"]["versionId"] = vid

    hist_r = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/_history",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )
    assert hist_r.status_code == 200
    hist_vids = [
        e["resource"]["meta"]["versionId"]
        for e in hist_r.json().get("entry", [])
    ]
    for v in version_ids:
        assert v in hist_vids, f"Version {v} missing from history"
